idea.hook: What if 95% of everything was MISSING?
facts: Only ~5% of the universe is 'normal matter' (stars, planets, gas) – everything we can see and touch.
About 27% is 'Dark Matter.' It has gravity but emits no light, holding galaxies together.
The remaining ~68% is 'Dark Energy,' a mysterious force accelerating the universe's expansion.
idea.payoff: We understand less than 5% of the universe's composition. The rest is an enduring cosmic mystery.
idea.title: The Universe is 95% Invisible! 🤯

scenes.text: What if 95% of everything was MISSING?
scenes.visual: Dark, deep space with subtle, twinkling stars. A faint, glowing question mark subtly forms and fades in the background.
A vibrant, brightly lit spiral galaxy (like Andromeda) slowly zooms into view, clearly showing stars and nebulae. Text indicates 'Stars, planets, gas. Everything we see.' implicitly.
A ghostly, swirling galaxy silhouette. Invisible force lines (subtle white arrows) suggest gravitational pull, holding visible stars in place without emitting light.
A cosmic web grid of points (representing galaxies) rapidly expands outwards from the center of the screen, emphasizing acceleration. Subtle red arrows reinforce expansion.

scenes.text: 95% of the Universe is a MYSTERY.
scenes.visual: A vast, awe-inspiring deep field cosmic image (like Hubble Ultra Deep Field) slowly zooms out, emphasizing the immense, unexplored unknown. A subtle, shimmering overlay hints at the unseen.
